keyPoints: Insertion sort is a simple and efficient sorting algorithm for small datasets
It works by dividing the list into smaller chunks and sorting each chunk individually
The algorithm has a time complexity of O(n^2) in the worst case, but can be O(n) in the best case
Insertion sort is stable, meaning that the order of equal elements is preserved
It is suitable for nearly sorted lists or small datasets
Insertion sort is not suitable for large datasets due to its high time complexity
